---
title: "Conclusions"
subtitle: "Cross-phase take-aways and scenario stress-tests"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .phase-c-derived { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr)); gap: 0.75rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
  .phase-c-derived .card { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0.6rem 0.75rem; background: #fff; }
  .phase-c-derived .card .label { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #555; }
  .phase-c-derived .card .value { font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 1.1rem; }

  /* Sticky sidebar + main-body layout, identical to phase-c.qmd. The
     left gutter's width is measured at runtime (see the <script> below)
     because Quarto's page grid places the body column off-centre. */
  .pc-section {
    --pc-gap: 1.5rem;
    --pc-left-gutter: 0px;
    --pc-sidebar-w: clamp(0px, calc(var(--pc-left-gutter) - 1rem), 420px);
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: var(--pc-sidebar-w) var(--pc-gap) minmax(0, 1fr);
    align-items: start;
    margin-left: calc(-1 * (var(--pc-sidebar-w) + var(--pc-gap)));
  }
  .pc-section > .pc-main { grid-column: 3; min-width: 0; }
  .pc-section > .pc-controls {
    grid-column: 1;
    position: sticky; top: 0.75rem;
    align-self: start;
    max-height: calc(100vh - 1.5rem);
    overflow-y: auto;
  }
  .pc-controls {
    padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem;
    border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
  }
  .pc-controls h3 { font-size: 0.9rem; margin: 0.9rem 0 0.4rem; color: #333; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; border-bottom: 1px solid #e0e0e0; padding-bottom: 0.2rem; }
  .pc-controls p { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #555; margin: 0.25rem 0 0.6rem; }
  .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] {
    align-items: center;
  }
  .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] > label {
    width: 11rem !important;
    min-width: 11rem !important;
    max-width: 11rem !important;
    white-space: normal !important;
    line-height: 1.2;
    flex-shrink: 0;
  }
  .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] input[type="number"],
  .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] select {
    min-width: 5.5rem;
  }
  .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] input[type="range"] {
    min-width: 0;
    flex: 1 1 auto;
  }
  @media (max-width: 1100px) {
    .pc-section { display: block; margin-left: 0; }
    .pc-section > .pc-controls { position: static; max-height: none; }
    .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] > label {
      width: auto !important;
      min-width: 90px !important;
      max-width: 160px !important;
    }
  }
  .pc-section.pc-narrow { display: block; margin-left: 0; }
  .pc-section.pc-narrow > .pc-controls { position: static; max-height: none; }
  .pc-section.pc-narrow .pc-controls form[class*="oi-"] > label {
    width: auto !important;
    min-width: 90px !important;
    max-width: 160px !important;
  }

  .pc-reset { margin-top: 1rem; padding-top: 0.75rem; border-top: 1px solid #e0e0e0; }
  .pc-reset button {
    width: 100%;
    padding: 0.4rem 0.7rem;
    border: 1px solid #888;
    background: #fff;
    color: #111;
    border-radius: 4px;
    cursor: pointer;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
    font-weight: 500;
  }
  .pc-reset button:hover { background: #eef; border-color: #555; }

  .pc-curve-box { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa; padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem; margin: 0.75rem 0; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.4rem; margin: 0 0 0.6rem; align-items: center; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button { padding: 0.3rem 0.7rem; border: 1px solid #888; background: #fff; color: #111; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 0.85rem; font-weight: 500; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar button:hover { background: #eef; border-color: #555; }
  .pc-curve-toolbar .hint { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #666; margin-left: auto; }
  .pc-curve-canvas { display: block; max-width: 100%; height: auto; touch-action: none; cursor: crosshair; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #ccc; border-radius: 6px; }
</style>
<script>
  (function () {
    var MIN_GUTTER = 320;
    function update() {
      document.querySelectorAll('.pc-section').forEach(function (sec) {
        var parent = sec.parentElement;
        if (!parent) return;
        var left = parent.getBoundingClientRect().left;
        if (left < MIN_GUTTER) {
          sec.classList.add('pc-narrow');
          sec.style.setProperty('--pc-left-gutter', '0px');
        } else {
          sec.classList.remove('pc-narrow');
          sec.style.setProperty('--pc-left-gutter', left + 'px');
        }
      });
    }
    var schedule = function () { requestAnimationFrame(update); };
    if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
      document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', schedule);
    } else {
      schedule();
    }
    window.addEventListener('load', schedule);
    window.addEventListener('resize', schedule);
  })();
</script>
```

[Phase B](phase-b.qmd) cross-checked the closed-form math against Monte
Carlo. [Phase C](phase-c.qmd) lets you drive the live simulator from
sliders. This page collects the cross-cutting take-aways of the
research programme and adds a deterministic **stress-test tool** — draw
any price trajectory you like, and the three books' P&L is recomputed
on that exact path.

## Business take-aways

A synthesis of what the Phase A math and the Phase B / Phase C
simulator runs agree on.

- **Fee vs. unhedged principal.** The fee book's revenue is always
  $\geq 0$ but proportional to the token price ($R_{\mathrm{fee}} = f
  \cdot P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$). The unhedged principal book has
  capped upside and structurally unbounded downside: it is short the
  token at a fixed quote $Q$.
- **Pre-buying inventory is the natural principal hedge.** Moving the
  pre-purchase from nothing toward full coverage shrinks every risk
  metric — variance collapses as $(1 - \tau)^2$ in Phase C's coverage
  parametrisation (equivalently $(1 - \alpha)^2$ in Phase A §3c), so
  SD and CVaR collapse linearly in $(1 - \tau)$. Tail risk reduction
  is therefore roughly proportional to coverage.
- **Cost basis is a location parameter, not a risk parameter.** Raising
  $C_{\mathrm{basis}}$ shifts the principal book's distribution left
  along the P&L axis by the difference; it leaves the shape, SD, VaR,
  and CVaR unchanged. This is why risk managers care about coverage
  $\tau$ far more than about the unrealised gain / loss on the
  existing inventory.
- **Over- and under-hedging produce differently-shaped loss tails.**
  Under-hedge ($k < N \cdot P$): residual exposure is $-P\lambda J_\tau$,
  so losses come from **high** prices. $J_\tau$ is an averaged
  log-normal with an unbounded upper tail, giving an unbounded but
  softened loss tail. Over-hedge ($k > N \cdot P$): residual exposure
  is $+k_\mathrm{left} \cdot S_T$, so losses come from **low** prices.
  Because $S_T \geq 0$, the loss tail is *bounded*; the heavy raw
  log-normal tail lands on the **gain** side, not the loss side.
  Crossing $k = N \cdot P$ therefore switches the loss tail from
  unbounded-but-averaged to bounded — changing the *kind* of risk, not
  just the amount.
- **$Q = Q^*$ equalises means, not distributions.** At the break-even
  quote $Q^* = (1 + f) \cdot P \cdot S_0 \cdot (e^{\mu T} - 1)/(\mu T)$
  the fee book and the back-to-back principal book have matching
  $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$. Their *distributions* are near-mirror images:
  same random kernel $I_T$, entered with opposite signs. The principal
  book trades mean preservation for a left-skewed loss tail — the
  single most important asymmetry this programme surfaces.

## Custom price curve scenario

The Monte Carlo in Phase C averages over a GBM's random futures.
Sometimes you want the opposite: fix **one** specific price trajectory
and see what the books do under it. Draw (or click-and-drag) a curve
below — a crash, a spike, a choppy flat — and the three book P&Ls are
recomputed deterministically from that trajectory in real time, using
the sliders in the left pane.

### Why a deterministic scenario matters alongside the MC

Monte Carlo output is a statement about averages and tails. It answers
"in the long run, what does the book earn?" and "how bad is the worst
5%?" — but it **smooths over every individual story**. A \$1 million
loss on a path where the token spiked in month 3 and crashed in month
7 gets averaged into the same CVaR number as a \$1 million loss from a
path that simply trended up slowly.

A single drawn-curve scenario is the complement: "given *this*
specific story, what would the book actually have done?" Concretely,
use it to:

- **Stress-test against named scenarios.** The toolbar has
  presets for a mid-horizon crash, a spike, a linear +50% / −50%
  drift, a random walk, and (when data is available) the actual
  historical kVCM price series. Each answers a business question
  that is sharp enough to bring to a meeting: *"if this happens, do
  we still have the capital to finish the year?"*
- **Validate intuition about the MC mean.** If the drawn P&L on a
  "flat at $S_0$" curve agrees with the closed-form expectation
  under $\mu = 0$, you know the integral path is wired correctly.
  If it doesn't, the MC number is suspect too.
- **Separate path-specific loss from path-averaged loss.** The MC
  mean is an expectation over all futures; the drawn-curve P&L is
  the value on one. Comparing the two (the "drawn − MC mean"
  column) tells you how much *this specific story* differs from the
  long-run average — which is what a stress test is trying to
  quantify.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## How the curve is used

The drawn values $\tilde S_0, \tilde S_1, \ldots, \tilde S_n$ define the
base trajectory. If the noise variance $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}}$ is
positive, each step is multiplied by an accumulated log-normal shock
$\exp\!\big(\sigma_{\mathrm{noise}}\sqrt{dt}\,Z_k - \tfrac12
\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}}\,dt\big)$ (reusing the seed slider so runs
are reproducible). Integrals on that realised path are computed by the
trapezoid rule ($I_T = \int_0^T S_t\,dt$, tail integral over $[\tau T,
T]$), and $S_T$ is read off the last point. All book formulas are
identical to the MC ones; only the price path differs.

**Practical tip.** Leaving $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} = 0$ gives a
clean, reproducible "what-if" result — the P&L you read off the table
below is exactly the P&L on the curve you see. Turning noise on
simulates "what if the trajectory I envisioned had typical day-to-day
jitter around it?" — useful when the question is robustness of a
narrative rather than the narrative itself.
:::

### The toolbar presets

Each of the buttons above the canvas installs a pre-baked shape. Use
them as starting points and then refine by hand.

- **Flat @ $S_0$.** The price never moves. Under $\mu = 0$ this is the
  mean path of GBM, so the three book P&Ls on this curve should be
  close to the MC means (modulo the Jensen inequality between
  $\mathbb{E}[I_T]$ and $T \cdot S_0$).
- **Linear +50% / −50%.** Constant-rate drift up or down. Good for
  sanity-checking how $\mu$ acts: a +50% linear rise produces a
  larger fee revenue, a larger principal loss, and a larger
  custom-book loss (via $J_\tau$) in predictable proportion.
- **Crash @ ½T.** Price stays flat, then collapses at the horizon's
  midpoint. Tests what happens when the operator is caught with
  exposure during a sudden re-rating. Particularly informative when
  coverage is close to but below 100% — the crash may land inside
  the uncovered tail window.
- **Spike @ ⅓T.** A transient rally early in the horizon that fades
  back. Tests the opposite scenario: a brief period of expensive
  tokens that the back-to-back book has to buy through.
- **Random walk.** A rough approximation of GBM drawn by hand — not
  the same as the simulator's MC, but visually similar. Useful as a
  "what might one realisation actually look like?" reference.
- **Historical.** If `npm run fetch:prices` has populated
  `data/kvcm-historical.json`, this button loads the real kVCM series
  (Alchemy Prices API) and auto-resizes the horizon to match the
  available history. The button is disabled when the file is missing
  or empty, and the rest of the canvas continues to work.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
historicalPrices = {
  // Fetched at build time by `npm run fetch:prices` (Alchemy Prices API).
  // Missing or empty file ⇒ the "Historical" preset below is inert.
  try {
    const r = await fetch("data/kvcm-historical.json");
    if (!r.ok) return { data: [] };
    return await r.json();
  } catch (e) {
    return { data: [] };
  }
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// "Today" anchor for the custom-curve x-axis. Prefers the fetchedAt
// timestamp baked into kvcm-historical.json by `npm run fetch:prices`
// so the rightmost tick reflects build time rather than page-open
// time; falls back to the current date if the build-time fetch is
// missing. Captured once per page load so ticks don't shift while the
// document is open.
buildDate = {
  const iso = historicalPrices && historicalPrices.fetchedAt;
  if (iso) {
    const parsed = new Date(iso);
    if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) return parsed;
  }
  return new Date();
}
```

:::: {.pc-section}

::: {.pc-controls}
The drawn-curve scenario depends on the inventory, contract, and
horizon sliders below. Drift $\mu$, variance $\sigma^2$, and jump
parameters ($\lambda_J$, $\mu_J$, $\sigma_J$) are fixed at the Phase C
defaults ($\mu = 0.05$, $\sigma^2 = 0.25$, no jumps) for the comparison
MC — open [Phase C](phase-c.qmd) to vary those.

### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof P = Inputs.range([100, 25000], { step: 10, value: 1790, label: "P (kVCM / tonne)" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Step = 1 (not 5): a coarser step causes a range-input snap when the
// value is set to `history.length - 1`, inflating nPoints past the
// available history and leaving a non-historical fill at the left edge.
viewof Tdays = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const fallback = 365;
  const dflt = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : fallback;
  return Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 1, value: dflt, label: "horizon T (days)" });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Curve settings

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveYMax = Inputs.range([0.01, 3], { step: 0.005, value: 0.2, label: "canvas y-max (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof curveVar = Inputs.range([0, 2.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0, label: "noise variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
conclusionsResetButton = {
  const hist = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const defaultTdays = hist.length >= 2
    ? Math.max(30, Math.min(1095, hist.length - 1))
    : 365;
  const defaults = {
    pi0: 125, P: 1790,
    lambdaDay: 1, kPre: 300000, cBasis: 21000,
    Tdays: defaultTdays, Q: 180, fee: 0.4,
    curveYMax: 0.2, curveVar: 0, seed: 42,
  };
  const views = {
    pi0: viewof pi0, P: viewof P,
    lambdaDay: viewof lambdaDay, kPre: viewof kPre, cBasis: viewof cBasis,
    Tdays: viewof Tdays, Q: viewof Q, fee: viewof fee,
    curveYMax: viewof curveYMax, curveVar: viewof curveVar, seed: viewof seed,
  };
  const wrap = document.createElement("div");
  wrap.className = "pc-reset";
  const btn = document.createElement("button");
  btn.type = "button";
  btn.textContent = "Reset to defaults";
  btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    for (const k of Object.keys(defaults)) {
      const el = views[k];
      el.value = defaults[k];
      el.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
    }
  });
  wrap.appendChild(btn);
  return wrap;
}
```

:::

::: {.pc-main}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function makeRng(seedVal) {
  let state = seedVal >>> 0;
  let cached = null;
  const uniform = () => {
    state = (state + 0x6d2b79f5) >>> 0;
    let t = state;
    t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1);
    t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61);
    const u = ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
    return u === 0 ? 1 / 4294967296 : u;
  };
  const normal = () => {
    if (cached !== null) {
      const z = cached;
      cached = null;
      return z;
    }
    const u1 = uniform();
    const u2 = uniform();
    const r = Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u1));
    const th = 2 * Math.PI * u2;
    cached = r * Math.sin(th);
    return r * Math.cos(th);
  };
  return { uniform, normal };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function expm1OverX(x) {
  if (x === 0) return 1;
  if (Math.abs(x) < 1e-8) return 1 + x / 2 + (x * x) / 6;
  return Math.expm1(x) / x;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function quantileSorted(sorted, q) {
  const n = sorted.length;
  if (n === 0) return NaN;
  if (n === 1) return sorted[0];
  const pos = q * (n - 1);
  const lo = Math.floor(pos);
  const hi = Math.ceil(pos);
  if (lo === hi) return sorted[lo];
  const frac = pos - lo;
  return sorted[lo] * (1 - frac) + sorted[hi] * frac;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function tailMean(sortedAscending, q) {
  const n = sortedAscending.length;
  const k = Math.max(1, Math.ceil((1 - q) * n));
  let s = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < k; i++) s += sortedAscending[i];
  return s / k;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
xTicksForHorizon = function (tdays) {
  let step;
  if (tdays <= 60) step = 7;
  else if (tdays <= 730) step = 30;
  else step = 365;
  const ticks = [];
  for (let d = 0; d <= tdays; d += step) ticks.push(d);
  return ticks;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
xTicksAnchoredRight = function (tdays) {
  let step;
  if (tdays <= 60) step = 7;
  else if (tdays <= 730) step = 30;
  else step = 365;
  const ticks = [];
  for (let d = tdays; d >= 0; d -= step) ticks.push(d);
  return ticks.reverse();
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
formatTickDate = function (date, tdays) {
  if (tdays <= 60) {
    return date.toLocaleString("en-US", { month: "short", day: "numeric" });
  }
  if (tdays <= 730) {
    return date.toLocaleString("en-US", { month: "short", year: "2-digit" });
  }
  return date.toLocaleString("en-US", { year: "numeric" });
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function simulate(inputs) {
  const { S0, mu, sigma, P, lambda, T, Q, fee, kPre, cBasis, nPaths, nSteps, seed } = inputs;
  const rng = makeRng(seed);
  const dt = T / nSteps;
  const drift = (mu - 0.5 * sigma * sigma) * dt;
  const diffusion = sigma * Math.sqrt(dt);
  const N = lambda * T;
  const tauFrac = (lambda > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lambda * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tokensUsedInternal = Math.min(kPre, lambda * T * P);
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lambda * T * P);
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * nSteps);
  const tailStartStep = tailStartRaw >= nSteps ? nSteps + 1 : tailStartRaw;

  const feeSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const principalSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const b2bSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const ITSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const terminalS = new Float64Array(nPaths);

  for (let i = 0; i < nPaths; i++) {
    let S = S0;
    let IT = 0;
    let tailInt = 0;
    IT += 0.5 * S;
    if (tailStartStep === 0) tailInt += 0.5 * S;
    for (let k = 1; k <= nSteps; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      S = S * Math.exp(drift + diffusion * z);
      const w = (k === nSteps) ? 0.5 : 1;
      IT += w * S;
      if (k >= tailStartStep) {
        const wt = (k === tailStartStep || k === nSteps) ? 0.5 : 1;
        tailInt += wt * S;
      }
    }
    IT *= dt;
    tailInt *= dt;
    terminalS[i] = S;
    ITSamples[i] = IT;
    feeSamples[i] = fee * P * lambda * IT;
    b2bSamples[i] = Q * N - P * lambda * IT;
    principalSamples[i] =
      Q * N - cBasis - P * lambda * tailInt + tokensLeftover * S;
  }

  return {
    feeSamples,
    principalSamples,
    b2bSamples,
    ITSamples,
    terminalS,
    tauFrac,
    tokensUsedInternal,
    tokensLeftover,
    N,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function summarise(samples) {
  const n = samples.length;
  let sum = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) sum += samples[i];
  const mean = sum / n;
  let sse = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
    const d = samples[i] - mean;
    sse += d * d;
  }
  const variance = n > 1 ? sse / (n - 1) : 0;
  const sd = Math.sqrt(variance);
  return { mean, sd };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const S0 = P > 0 ? pi0 / P : 0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  return { S0, T, lambda, N };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Self-contained comparison MC for the drawn-curve table. Drift and
// variance are fixed at the Phase C defaults; open Phase C to vary
// them. nPaths / nSteps are hardcoded at Phase C's defaults too.
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0: derived.S0, mu: 0.05, sigma: Math.sqrt(0.25), P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    nPaths: 5000, nSteps: 100, seed,
  };
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    principal: summarise(raw.principalSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof customCurve = {
  // One sample per day. The rightmost point is the build-time anchor
  // (values[nPoints-1] = today) and the leftmost is (Tdays) days ago,
  // so moving the horizon slider extends or trims the left edge
  // without reshaping the already-drawn right edge.
  const TDaysVal = Tdays;
  const nPoints = TDaysVal + 1;
  const width = 820;
  const height = 320;
  const margin = { top: 10, right: 12, bottom: 32, left: 58 };
  const plotW = width - margin.left - margin.right;
  const plotH = height - margin.top - margin.bottom;
  const yMax = curveYMax;
  const S0Val = derived.S0;
  const history = (historicalPrices && Array.isArray(historicalPrices.data))
    ? historicalPrices.data
    : [];
  const sigmaN = Math.sqrt(curveVar);
  const dt = derived.T / (nPoints - 1);
  const seedVal = seed;

  // Persist the drawn curve indexed by days-ago (0 = build-time anchor)
  // so shrinking and regrowing the horizon restores the previously
  // drawn samples at the leftmost part of the curve. NaN marks "never
  // drawn" so π₀/P slider changes still refresh the initial fill (via
  // the derived S₀) for days that haven't been touched.
  const MAX_TDAYS = 1095;
  const masterKey = "__conclusionsCustomCurveByDaysAgo";
  let master = window[masterKey];
  if (!(master instanceof Float64Array) || master.length !== MAX_TDAYS + 1) {
    master = new Float64Array(MAX_TDAYS + 1);
    master.fill(NaN);
    window[masterKey] = master;
  }

  // Seed the canvas with the historical series on first page load. The
  // one-shot window flag is required: without it, every slider-driven
  // re-run of this cell would overwrite user edits.
  const seededKey = "__conclusionsCustomCurveSeeded";
  if (!window[seededKey]) {
    window[seededKey] = true;
    if (history.length >= 2) {
      const fillCount = Math.min(history.length, MAX_TDAYS + 1);
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo < fillCount; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[history.length - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
    }
  }

  const values = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
    const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
    const stored = master[daysAgo];
    values[i] = isNaN(stored) ? S0Val : stored;
  }

  const container = document.createElement("div");
  container.className = "pc-curve-box";

  const toolbar = document.createElement("div");
  toolbar.className = "pc-curve-toolbar";
  const hint = document.createElement("span");
  hint.className = "hint";
  hint.textContent = "Click and drag on the canvas to draw a price path.";

  const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
  canvas.className = "pc-curve-canvas";
  canvas.width = width;
  canvas.height = height;
  const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");

  const indexToX = (i) => margin.left + (i / (nPoints - 1)) * plotW;
  const priceToY = (p) => margin.top + (1 - p / yMax) * plotH;
  const xToIndex = (x) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(nPoints - 1, Math.round(((x - margin.left) / plotW) * (nPoints - 1))));
  const yToPrice = (y) =>
    Math.max(0, Math.min(yMax, yMax - ((y - margin.top) / plotH) * yMax));

  const realised = new Float64Array(nPoints);
  function refreshRealised() {
    if (sigmaN === 0) {
      for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) realised[k] = values[k];
      return;
    }
    const rng = makeRng(seedVal);
    let logNoise = 0;
    realised[0] = values[0];
    for (let k = 1; k < nPoints; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      logNoise += sigmaN * Math.sqrt(dt) * z - 0.5 * sigmaN * sigmaN * dt;
      realised[k] = values[k] * Math.exp(logNoise);
    }
  }

  const DAY_MS = 86400000;
  const anchor = buildDate;
  const dateForDay = (d) => new Date(anchor.getTime() - (TDaysVal - d) * DAY_MS);
  const xTicks = xTicksAnchoredRight(TDaysVal);
  function redraw() {
    refreshRealised();
    ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height);
    ctx.strokeStyle = "#e5e5e5";
    ctx.lineWidth = 1;
    ctx.beginPath();
    for (let g = 1; g < 5; g++) {
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, y);
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, y);
    }
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      if (d <= 0 || d >= TDaysVal) continue;
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      ctx.moveTo(x, margin.top);
      ctx.lineTo(x, margin.top + plotH);
    }
    ctx.stroke();

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#333";
    ctx.strokeRect(margin.left, margin.top, plotW, plotH);

    ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
    ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
    ctx.textAlign = "right";
    ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
    for (let g = 0; g <= 5; g++) {
      const priceVal = yMax * (1 - g / 5);
      const y = margin.top + (g * plotH) / 5;
      ctx.fillText("$" + priceVal.toFixed(3), margin.left - 6, y);
    }
    ctx.textBaseline = "top";
    for (const d of xTicks) {
      const x = margin.left + (d / TDaysVal) * plotW;
      const isRight = d === TDaysVal;
      const isLeft = d === 0;
      ctx.textAlign = isRight ? "right" : isLeft ? "left" : "center";
      ctx.fillText(formatTickDate(dateForDay(d), TDaysVal), x, margin.top + plotH + 6);
    }

    ctx.font = "12px sans-serif";
    ctx.save();
    ctx.translate(14, margin.top + plotH / 2);
    ctx.rotate(-Math.PI / 2);
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("S_t (USD / kVCM)", 0, 0);
    ctx.restore();
    ctx.textAlign = "center";
    ctx.fillText("date", margin.left + plotW / 2, height - 2);

    if (S0Val > 0 && S0Val <= yMax) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.setLineDash([4, 3]);
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(margin.left, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.lineTo(margin.left + plotW, priceToY(S0Val));
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.setLineDash([]);
      ctx.fillStyle = "#2a7";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "bottom";
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.fillText("S₀", margin.left + 4, priceToY(S0Val) - 2);
    }

    if (sigmaN > 0) {
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#4c78a8";
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.7;
      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      ctx.lineJoin = "round";
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, realised[0]))));
      for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) {
        ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(Math.min(yMax, Math.max(0, realised[i]))));
      }
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
    }

    ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
    ctx.lineWidth = 2;
    ctx.lineJoin = "round";
    ctx.beginPath();
    ctx.moveTo(indexToX(0), priceToY(values[0]));
    for (let i = 1; i < nPoints; i++) ctx.lineTo(indexToX(i), priceToY(values[i]));
    ctx.stroke();

    if (sigmaN > 0) {
      const lx = margin.left + plotW - 140;
      const ly = margin.top + 8;
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.85)";
      ctx.fillRect(lx - 6, ly - 2, 140, 34);
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#ccc";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1;
      ctx.strokeRect(lx - 6, ly - 2, 140, 34);
      ctx.font = "11px ui-monospace, monospace";
      ctx.textAlign = "left";
      ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#c94";
      ctx.lineWidth = 2;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(lx, ly + 5);
      ctx.lineTo(lx + 18, ly + 5);
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
      ctx.fillText("drawn (base)", lx + 22, ly + 5);
      ctx.strokeStyle = "#4c78a8";
      ctx.lineWidth = 1.5;
      ctx.globalAlpha = 0.7;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.moveTo(lx, ly + 21);
      ctx.lineTo(lx + 18, ly + 21);
      ctx.stroke();
      ctx.globalAlpha = 1;
      ctx.fillStyle = "#333";
      ctx.fillText("realised (+ noise)", lx + 22, ly + 21);
    }
  }

  function emit() {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      master[nPoints - 1 - i] = values[i];
    }
    container.value = {
      values: Float64Array.from(values),
      realised: Float64Array.from(realised),
      nPoints,
    };
    container.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
  }

  let lastIdx = null;
  let drawing = false;

  function apply(ev) {
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = ((ev.clientX - rect.left) * canvas.width) / rect.width;
    const y = ((ev.clientY - rect.top) * canvas.height) / rect.height;
    const idx = xToIndex(x);
    const price = yToPrice(y);
    if (lastIdx === null || lastIdx === idx) {
      values[idx] = price;
    } else {
      const lo = Math.min(idx, lastIdx);
      const hi = Math.max(idx, lastIdx);
      const loP = lastIdx <= idx ? values[lastIdx] : price;
      const hiP = lastIdx <= idx ? price : values[lastIdx];
      for (let k = lo; k <= hi; k++) {
        const frac = (k - lo) / (hi - lo);
        values[k] = loP * (1 - frac) + hiP * frac;
      }
    }
    lastIdx = idx;
  }

  canvas.addEventListener("pointerdown", (ev) => {
    ev.preventDefault();
    drawing = true;
    lastIdx = null;
    canvas.setPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId);
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  canvas.addEventListener("pointermove", (ev) => {
    if (!drawing) return;
    ev.preventDefault();
    apply(ev);
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const endStroke = (ev) => {
    drawing = false;
    lastIdx = null;
    if (canvas.hasPointerCapture?.(ev.pointerId)) canvas.releasePointerCapture(ev.pointerId);
  };
  canvas.addEventListener("pointerup", endStroke);
  canvas.addEventListener("pointercancel", endStroke);

  function setPreset(fn) {
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = fn(i / (nPoints - 1));
    redraw();
    emit();
  }
  function addButton(label, fn) {
    const b = document.createElement("button");
    b.type = "button";
    b.textContent = label;
    b.addEventListener("click", fn);
    toolbar.appendChild(b);
  }
  addButton("Flat @ S₀", () => setPreset(() => S0Val));
  addButton("Linear +50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 + 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Linear −50%", () => setPreset((u) => S0Val * (1 - 0.5 * u)));
  addButton("Crash @ ½T", () =>
    setPreset((u) => (u < 0.5 ? S0Val : S0Val * Math.max(0.05, 1 - 1.6 * (u - 0.5))))
  );
  addButton("Spike @ ⅓T", () => {
    const peak = S0Val * 2.5;
    setPreset((u) => {
      const d = u - 1 / 3;
      return S0Val + (peak - S0Val) * Math.exp(-80 * d * d);
    });
  });
  addButton("Random walk", () => {
    let s = S0Val;
    const vals = [];
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      s = Math.max(0, s * (1 + 0.02 * (Math.random() - 0.5)));
      vals.push(s);
    }
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) values[i] = vals[i];
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  const historicalBtn = document.createElement("button");
  historicalBtn.type = "button";
  historicalBtn.textContent = history.length >= 2 ? "Historical" : "Historical (unavailable)";
  historicalBtn.disabled = history.length < 2;
  historicalBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    // Right-align the daily historical series: the most recent sample
    // lands on today (rightmost point) and earlier samples fill back
    // in toward the left.
    const dayCount = history.length;
    const targetTdays = Math.max(30, Math.min(MAX_TDAYS, dayCount - 1));
    const tdaysView = viewof Tdays;

    if (Number(tdaysView.value) !== targetTdays) {
      // Stash the series into master so the cell's re-run — triggered
      // by the Tdays change below — picks it up without a second fill.
      for (let daysAgo = 0; daysAgo <= targetTdays; daysAgo++) {
        master[daysAgo] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
      }
      tdaysView.value = targetTdays;
      tdaysView.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", { bubbles: true }));
      return;
    }

    // Idempotent re-click: Tdays already at target, so redraw in place.
    for (let i = 0; i < nPoints; i++) {
      const daysAgo = nPoints - 1 - i;
      values[i] = history[dayCount - 1 - daysAgo].value;
    }
    redraw();
    emit();
  });
  toolbar.appendChild(historicalBtn);
  toolbar.appendChild(hint);

  container.appendChild(toolbar);
  container.appendChild(canvas);

  redraw();
  container.value = {
    values: Float64Array.from(values),
    realised: Float64Array.from(realised),
    nPoints,
  };
  return container;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customScenario = {
  const { values: base, realised, nPoints } = customCurve;
  const T = derived.T;
  const lam = derived.lambda;
  const N = derived.N;
  const dt = T / (nPoints - 1);

  let IT = 0;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === 0 || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    IT += w * realised[k];
  }
  IT *= dt;

  const tauFrac = (lam > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lam * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * (nPoints - 1));
  const tailStartIdx = tailStartRaw >= nPoints - 1 ? nPoints : tailStartRaw;
  let tailInt = 0;
  for (let k = tailStartIdx; k < nPoints; k++) {
    const w = (k === tailStartIdx || k === nPoints - 1) ? 0.5 : 1;
    tailInt += w * realised[k];
  }
  tailInt *= dt;

  const ST = realised[nPoints - 1];
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lam * T * P);
  const feeRev = fee * P * lam * IT;
  const b2b = Q * N - P * lam * IT;
  const cust = Q * N - cBasis - P * lam * tailInt + tokensLeftover * ST;

  let maxS = -Infinity, minS = Infinity;
  for (let k = 0; k < nPoints; k++) {
    if (realised[k] > maxS) maxS = realised[k];
    if (realised[k] < minS) minS = realised[k];
  }
  const meanS = IT / T;

  return { base, realised, nPoints, IT, tailInt, ST, tauFrac, tokensLeftover, feeRev, b2b, cust, maxS, minS, meanS };
}
```

_Orange: the curve you drew. Blue (when $\sigma^2_{\mathrm{noise}} > 0$):
the realised noisy path actually fed into the integrals, overlaid on the
same canvas. Change the PRNG seed slider to cycle through different noise
realisations._

### Curve summary and deterministic P&L

The next two blocks present the **result** of whatever you drew:

1. A summary card strip with the min / max of the realised path, its
   mean (which equals $I_T / T$ and is therefore the one-point summary
   of the path most relevant to the fee book), the terminal price
   $S_T$, and the tail integral $J_\tau$.
2. A deterministic P&L table for all three books on the drawn
   trajectory, alongside the MC mean for each book and the difference.
   Reading the difference column left-to-right tells you "how much is
   this specific story a good / bad outcome relative to the long-run
   average?"

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">min / max drawn S</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.minS.toFixed(4)} / ${"$" + customScenario.maxS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">mean drawn S (I_T / T)</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.meanS.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">terminal S_T</div><div class="value">${"$" + customScenario.ST.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tail integral ∫_{τT}^{T} S_t dt</div><div class="value">${customScenario.tailInt.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
customRows = [
  { book: "fee",                      pnl: customScenario.feeRev, mcMean: run.fee.mean,       diff: customScenario.feeRev - run.fee.mean },
  { book: "principal (custom)",       pnl: customScenario.cust,   mcMean: run.principal.mean, diff: customScenario.cust   - run.principal.mean },
  { book: "principal (back-to-back)", pnl: customScenario.b2b,    mcMean: run.b2b.mean,       diff: customScenario.b2b    - run.b2b.mean },
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(customRows, {
  columns: ["book", "pnl", "mcMean", "diff"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    pnl: "P&L on drawn curve",
    mcMean: "E[Π] (MC)",
    diff: "drawn − MC mean",
  },
  format: {
    pnl: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    mcMean: x => (x >= 0 ? "$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
    diff: x => (x >= 0 ? "+$" : "−$") + Math.abs(x).toFixed(2),
  },
})
```

_The **drawn** column is the deterministic P&L implied by the trajectory
you sketched; the **MC mean** column is $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$ from a
self-contained Monte Carlo at the Phase C defaults ($\mu = 0.05$,
$\sigma^2 = 0.25$, no jumps, 5,000 paths); the **diff** shows how this
specific scenario compares to the average across random GBM futures._

**How to use this table.**

- A **positive diff on the fee row** means you drew a high-price path:
  the fee book earned more revenue on this curve than it does on
  average. Because the fee book's P&L is proportional to $I_T$, you
  can read the diff column as a linear function of $\bar S - \bar
  S_{\mathrm{MC}}$, where $\bar S = I_T / T$.
- A **negative diff on the principal (custom) row** when
  coverage is partial means the drawn curve is *worse* for the
  operator than average: the token was more expensive over the
  uncovered tail window $[\tau T, T]$ than the GBM expects on average.
  This is the natural stress-test read-out.
- A **diff of roughly zero on all three rows at once** is the
  signature of a drawn curve that is "typical" — close to the mean
  path of GBM. That tells you the curve is a plausible but
  unexceptional future, not a stress scenario.
- **Coverage matters.** If the custom book shows no sensitivity to
  the drawn curve (diff ≈ 0 even for wild shapes), check that
  coverage is below 100%. At 100% coverage the custom book is
  deterministic and its P&L does not depend on the path at all.

:::

::::

## Scope & limitations

What the current research programme does *not* cover (yet):

- **Price dynamics are GBM with an optional Merton jump-diffusion
  overlay.** The jump sliders are now live on [Phase C](phase-c.qmd);
  regime switching, Poisson retirement flow, historical calibration of
  $(\mu, \sigma)$ against a kVCM proxy, and dynamic hedging strategies
  are the remaining Phase C roadmap items. Each will swap in behind
  the same UI.
- **Gas, slippage, and discounting are not modelled.** Each introduces
  a small correction to the book formulas (typically a few percent),
  not a qualitative change in shape. They are not yet exposed in the
  simulator's parameter record (`src/params.ts`).
- **Nothing here is financial advice.** Use the framework; plug in
  your own $(\mu, \sigma)$ calibrated from data before any business
  decision; treat the drawn-curve results as illustrative scenarios,
  not forecasts.

## Implementation notes

- **Performance.** The Phase C simulator runs sub-second at 5,000
  paths × 100 steps on a typical laptop. The comparison MC on this
  page uses those same defaults.
- **Reproducibility.** The PRNG is a seeded Mulberry32, shared by
  construction across Phase B (`src/rng.ts`), Phase C, and this page's
  duplicated inline port. A given `(seed, inputs)` pair is
  deterministic across reloads — both for the Monte Carlo and for the
  noise overlay on the drawn curve.
- **What is still Phase B under the hood.** Deterministic retirement
  flow. (GBM dynamics now have an optional jump-diffusion overlay on
  [Phase C](phase-c.qmd).) Future Phase C iterations will replace the
  remaining simplifications — regime switching and Poisson demand —
  while keeping the same UI and book definitions.
- **Why the simulator code is duplicated here.** The drawn-curve
  comparison column needs live Monte Carlo means, and future scenarios
  (jump-diffusion, regime switching) will not have closed-form anchors
  — so a self-contained MC on this page is forward-compatible. The
  duplication is intentional for now; a factored OJS module would be a
  natural refactor once a third page needs the same helpers.
